body_txt: Sometimes the criminals make the best heroes. They don't have reservations on certain things such as "morals" and they have no qualms with going around the proper channels to accomplish their goals. But since they also have skills that have gone around the more natural education system , they are brought in as specialists. There is practically a built-in subtrope here specifically where hackers are brought in to do some uber-hacking for the good guys. Super-Trope to Boxed Crook , when they are offered immediate freedom upon succeeding at a Suicide Mission , and To Catch Heroes, Hire Villains , tasking The Hero 's former foes to bring them to justice. Compare Trading Bars for Stripes , where freedom is offered after going through a tour of military duty (although in that case, it is more because they are "expendable" than just because of their skills). See also Condemned Contestant , Doctor von Turncoat , Consulting a Convicted Killer . Contrast Reformed Criminal , when the character serves the law because of a genuine Heel–Face Turn . One inversion is From Camouflage to Criminal , where a member of the military turns to a life of crime after leaving the service; when the soldier-turned-criminal is the protagonist (especially one who was persecuted or wrongfully convicted, e.g., Rambo , The A-Team ), expect this to get doubly inverted with their former commander trying to make a deal with them. Another inversion is Hired by the Oppressor , when a group of Hired Guns are hired by, well, an oppressive faction, for certain goals, and rewarded. Sometimes overlaps with the Token Evil Teammate , depending on how self-serving they actually are.
